overview.description: Multi-agent robotics research lab at FIT CTU Prague specialising in multi-agent pathfinding and collective robot motion planning
overview.description_long: Robotic Agents Laboratory (RoboAgeLab) is a research group at the Faculty of Information Technology, Czech Technical University in Prague. The lab focuses on intelligent decision-making of robotic agents in multi-agent scenarios, covering the full planning-to-execution pipeline: from abstract classical planning through motion planning to physical execution with real multi-robot systems. Research is grounded in symbolic AI — constraint satisfaction, heuristic search, SAT/SMT-based compilation, and classical/motion planning — with emphasis on explainable and safe AI. Application domains include swarm quadcopter pathfinding (Crazyflie drones), collective construction in Minecraft, and OZOBOT Evo robot swarms.
overview.tagline: Intelligent multi-agent robotics through symbolic AI

history.origin_story: Established within the Department of Applied Mathematics at FIT CTU Prague, growing from Pavel Surynek's multi-agent pathfinding research funded by the Czech Science Foundation.

key_milestones.event: First Czech Science Foundation project launched: Intelligent Algorithms for Generalized Variants of Multi-Agent Path Finding (2019–2021)
Paper on Multi-Agent Path Finding with Mutex Propagation published at ICAPS 2020
ESO-MAPF platform paper published at AAAI 2021
Second Czech Science Foundation project launched: logicMOVE (2022–2024)
FIT CTU student awarded Josef Hlávka Prize 2024 for outstanding results in collective robotics AI

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group at a public university; not a commercial entity competing in a product market.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Research lab funded by institutional grants (Czech Science Foundation); not subject to commercial market risk.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Consistently funded through Czech Science Foundation (GAČR) grants, with two consecutive multi-year projects (2019–2021, 2022–2024). Institutional backing from CTU/FIT provides stable operating base.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: As a university research group within FIT CTU, commercial risk metrics are not applicable. The lab has stable academic funding and produces peer-reviewed publications at top AI venues (AAAI, ICAPS, IJCAI).

metadata.notes: Research group within the Department of Applied Mathematics, Faculty of Information Technology, Czech Technical University in Prague. PI is prof. RNDr. Pavel Surynek, Ph.D., a leading expert in multi-agent pathfinding (MAPF) with publications at AAAI, ICAPS, and IJCAI. Lab room: TH:A-1346, Thákurova 7, Prague 6. Founded year estimated as 2019 based on first GAČR project start date.
